finding: The atom-ID and atom-CID gates treated equal strings as unconditional write/write freezes even after intent shape, lease, and shared-surface gates had established that two transactions wrote no common file or shared surface. This blocked independent work such as TASK-TEAM-0075 and TASK-AAO-0160.
expectedBehavior: CID write ownership is a semantic refinement of a common writable surface, not a global mutex. Preserve read-set visibility, but require a common target file or shared surface before a CID write conflict can freeze admission.
evidenceOrRepro: Source-first import/claim of TASK-AAO-0160 reported no shared paths but froze on `atom-cli-router` / `atom-validator-framework` CID overlap held by active 0075.
followUp: Regression now proves broad CID on disjoint files is `parallel-safe`, while same CID on the same target file remains `blocked-cid-conflict`; retain both decision and seven-layer matrix cases.
